what_it_does: Provides a read-first orientation for the repository, framing it as an addressed, typed, queryable substrate and listing navigation rules, substrate files, derivation tools, concern-based locations, and governing principles.
observations: Defines every folder and file as an address with a type and typed edges.
Directs agents to resolve location questions through docs/WAYFINDER.md before searching.
Describes coverage scans as territory-based and registry-count-driven.
States that structural map files are derived and should be regenerated after file changes.
Lists registry, wayfinder, graph, architecture, and tool files that make up the substrate.
Includes a concern-to-location quick map for tokens, grammar, canon, renderers, graph, icons, specimens, server, and thinking docs.
Names laws such as derive-never-place, registry-is-truth, equal-and-opposite typed edges, and system-not-the-use.
standouts: Treats paths as typed addresses rather than passive file locations.
Separates objective coverage reporting from judgement.
Emphasizes derived structure over hand-maintained structure.
conventions: Uses bullets and a monospace concern map for navigational compression.
Frames local project structure through registry-derived artifacts.
concerns: Assumes readers already understand terms such as substrate, census, ghost nodes, and COMPANY layer.
Several referenced files and tools are named without local definitions in this file.
notes: Brief someone that this is the repository orientation and navigation contract for working through its address substrate.
questions: What exact command sequence re-runs the full substrate after a file change?
What distinguishes authored meaning from derived structure in practice?
What is the expected agent workflow after reading the wayfinder?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated read-first purpose matches the content: it orients agents to the repository model, navigation rules, substrate artifacts, and principles.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make agents navigate and report through a declared substrate model instead of ad hoc search, while keeping derived maps synchronized with repository changes.
novelty: The file presents a repository as a typed address graph with registry-derived navigation surfaces and explicit agent rules for coverage scanning.
contribution: Contributes an agent-facing contract for treating the project as a typed address substrate, including navigation rules, registry artifacts, derivation tools, concern locations, and governing principles that a fused system would preserve as orientation metadata.
summary_for_embedding: This file is the read-first repository guide that defines counterpart/design as an addressed, typed, queryable substrate with rules, registry artifacts, derivation tools, concern maps, and structural laws.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to guide agents toward map-based navigation, complete territory-based coverage, and regeneration of derived substrate artifacts after structural changes.
